title: Intent routing is live across the Infinex account
deployed_facts: Users state what they want; the account chooses the path
Cross-chain swaps, bridges, and gas top-ups resolve from a single instruction
The user signs the destination, not the route
Routes are selected from multiple solvers; the chosen route is shown after the fact
The user never sees a chain selector for a swap that crosses chains
Failed routes do not require user intervention to re-route
headline: State the outcome. The account does the rest.
tier_reason: First product surface where the account-as-agent assumption is taken to its conclusion: the user no longer picks rails. The mental model of crypto changes for anyone who uses this. Reserved-template because it retires the chain selector — a UI element older than most chains.
